properties.remark: Willacy County EM reported one power pole and lines down at 1425 N 490 in Raymondville.
Correction: Willacy County EM reported one power pole and lines down at 1425 N 490 in Raymondville. Time radar estimated.
Rincon Del San Jose recorded a wind gust of 55 knots at 4:36 AM as thunderstorms moved through the area.
Buoy station RSJT2 Rincon Del San Jose, TX.
Relayed through Zapata S.O.
Web Report. Along FM 755 North of town.
Web Report: unknow how far north of Rio Grande City.
Web Report Along FM 490 near Faysville.
NIckel to quarter sized hail was reported. Estimated by resident and by video. Time estimated by video and radar.
Strong thunderstorm wind gusts began at 7:54 PM CDT and continued until 8:36 PM.
At least 3 power poles reported down along east of I-69E between Rancho Viejo and Olimito.
Strong thunderstorm wind gusts started at 8:36 PM CDT and continued until 10:00 PM CDT. Lasting about 1 hour and 24 minutes.
Video showed hail-dominant precipitation along P.J. Garza Road near E. Expressway 83.
Video showed quarter-sized hail near intersection of La Homa Road and Mile 7 Road north of Mission. Time and duration estimated by radar.
Quarter sized hail near intersection of Tom Gill and 3 Mile Line Road. Duration was 5 minutes. Video evidence provided.
Quarter sized hail briefly covered the ground in the vicinity of Doffing Rd. and Conway Ave. in north Mission.

Former cooperative observer reported quarter sized hail briefly covering the ground just south of the intersection of 7 mile and N. Doffing Road in Citrus City. Time estimated from radar and nearby reports.
Quarter to ping-pong ball sized hail near intersection of McColl and Military Highway...US 281. Duration of hail was fourteen minutes.
A few golfball sized stones than hail sized dropped a bit smaller...size unknown. Lasted between 10 and 15 minutes.
Emergency Management reports trees down in Mission, TX near Solar and Francisco. Radar estimated time.
Emergency Manager reported a downed powerline. Time estimated on radar.
Emergency manager reported a tree on a car.
Emergency Manager reported a downed powerline.

Small business La Michoacana Paleteria y Neveria had visible damage to the exterior wall and roofing. A submitted Facebook photo of the damage was later confirmed by the broadcast media and emergency management.
Buoy at Realitos Penusula recorded a thunderstorm wind gust of 34 kts. Thunderstorm wind gust associated with Tropical Storm Alberto.
City of Combes shared picture of landspout northeast of Woods Road just before 3 pm. Radar estimated time and location.

Radar estimates of 4 to 6 inches of rainfall over a 3 hour period. Flooding reported with water as high as 2 feet flowing over Highway 16 in the city of Zapata. Water reported in some home in the Medina Addtion.
Standing water in intersection of Pablo Kisel Blvd and Morrison Rd. Nearly impassable.
5.48 inches of rain fell in 90 minutes.

Harlingen PD received reports of flooded roads at the following locations: Fair Park Blvd, intersection of 7th and M St, Spur 5 and Frontage, Eye St, intersection of J St and Fair Park Blvd, Bowie Ave and 25th St, Frontage Rd. at Primera Rd exit to Wilson Rd, Le Moyne Garden, and Emerald Lake Dr. TX DoT has been notified and road closures are possible. Water halfway up cars reported in some spots.
Vehicle water rescue.
San Benito PD reports floods across much of the town, including: Franklin Rd, Woodford St, and Bowie St. Water entering homes and one water rescue necessary on Bowie St. Water is knee deep in places.
Water entering homes on Burr Oak Loop.
Water rescue on 11th street in downtown Brownsville.
Water entering homes on Agave Ave.
Vehicle water rescue.
Water entering house on Melva Ct.

Brownsville PD reported water on roads at the following locations: Frontage Rd and Alton Gloor Blvd, Old Alice and Bernard. Brownsville PD says most reports are coming from northern Brownsville and west of I-69E.
Recorded along line of showers just behind cold front.
